draft.conclusion: 评审结论：逐项核验完成，可以合并。

findings.problem: In Python regex, $ can match before a trailing newline while \Z only matches at the absolute end of the string, so 'joe\n' now fails validation. \Z 换行 锚 末尾 换行 $ newline trailing newline newline anchor
findings.evidence: re.search(r'^[\w.@+-]+$', 'joe\n') matches because $ tolerates one final newline; with \Z there is no match, so RegexValidator raises ValidationError. This is exactly the reported bug and the minimal fix. validationerror regexvalidator
findings.impact: re.search(r'^[\w.@+-]+$', 'joe\n') matches because $ tolerates one final newline; with \Z there is no match, so RegexValidator raises ValidationError. This is exactly the reported bug and the minimal fix. validationerror regexvalidator
findings.fix: No change needed (verify by running 'joe\n' through both validators before and after the patch). validators running

findings.problem: The character class and the ^ start anchor are unchanged; the only behavioral change is rejecting values that matched solely because of $'s newline tolerance. 字符集 不变 合法 通过 正常 用户名 character class unchanged valid pass valid unaffected
findings.evidence: Without re.MULTILINE, ^ already anchors at the true string start and [\w.@+-]+ is untouched, so every previously valid username still passes; there is no regression for existing users. re.multiline previously
findings.impact: Without re.MULTILINE, ^ already anchors at the true string start and [\w.@+-]+ is untouched, so every previously valid username still passes; there is no regression for existing users. re.multiline previously
findings.fix: No change needed (spot-check normal usernames such as 'joe', 'jean-marc', 'René' against both validators). spot-check validators

draft.tests: Both ASCIIUsernameValidator and UnicodeUsernameValidator get the same anchor change, and each gains a trailing-newline entry in the invalid_usernames regression tests. The two classes share the same pattern and Unicode is the default validator; fixing only one would leave the bug half-open. The new entries assert ValidationError and fail on the old regex, so they are trustworthy regression tests. 两个 validator 两个 校验 ASCII Unicode both validator invalid_usernames
